description.task: Given coin denominations and a target amount, find the minimum number of coins needed to make that amount. If the amount cannot be made, return -1. Use bottom-up DP.
instructions: for(int amt=1;amt<=amount;amt++) for(int coin:coins) if(coin<=amt&&dp[amt-coin]+1<dp[amt]) dp[amt]=dp[amt-coin]+1;
description.hint: for(int amt=1;amt<=amount;amt++) for(int coin:coins) if(coin<=amt&&dp[amt-coin]+1<dp[amt]) dp[amt]=dp[amt-coin]+1;
description.expectedOutput:
Coins [1,2,5], amount=11: 3
Coins [2],      amount=3:  -1
Coins [1,5,10], amount=18: 5
Coins [2,5,10], amount=3:  -1

description.task: Find the length of the Longest Increasing Subsequence (LIS) in an array. An increasing subsequence means each element is strictly greater than the previous. Use O(n²) DP where dp[i] = LIS ending at index i.
instructions: Find max: for(int x:dp) if(x>max) max=x;
[7,7,7,7]: no strictly increasing pair → LIS length 1
description.hint: dp[i]=Math.max(dp[i],dp[j]+1); then for(int x:dp) if(x>max) max=x;

description.task: You are a robber planning to rob houses along a street. Adjacent houses cannot be robbed on the same night. Given an array of non-negative integers representing the amount of money in each house, find the maximum amount you can rob without alerting the police.
instructions: Both methods must return the same result
